reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the target sentence '救命啊！我被困在这里了！'. Regarding the specific instruction 'extremely high pitch' (极高音), the audio demonstrates a significant upward shift in fundamental frequency, resulting in a falsetto-like, piercing tone that fits the description of 'extremely high' perfectly. The delivery also captures the urgency and distress required by the semantic content ('Help! I'm trapped!'). The pitch modulation is consistent throughout the utterance. The articulation is clear despite the extreme vocal register.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the content '救命啊，我被困在这里了', but it suffers from severe hallucination/repetition, saying the phrase approximately five times in a loop. Regarding the target dimension 'extremely high pitch' (极高音), the model completely fails to adhere to the instruction. The voice remains in a standard, comfortable female mid-range pitch. There is no attempt to raise the pitch to an 'extreme' level. Consequently, it fails both the content control (due to repetition) and the specific paralinguistic style request.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) followed the user's specific instruction to use an 'extremely high pitch' very well. The resulting audio is high-pitched and conveys the urgency of the text. Model 2 (T2) failed on multiple fronts: strictly speaking, it did not modify the pitch to be 'extremely high' (it used a normal speaking pitch), and it also produced a severe generation artifact by looping the sentence multiple times. T1 is the clear winner as it complied with the stylistic constraint, whereas T2 ignored it.
